what: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: pTRAPPING R package provides streamlined, reproducible workflow analysing TRAP-seq PhosphoTRAP RNA-sequencing data. wraps statistical power edgeR limma behind compact set purpose-built functions take raw counts matrix publication-ready tables figures just lines code. TRAP-seq? Translating Ribosome Affinity Purification followed sequencing (TRAP-seq) captures mRNAs actively translated specific cell type, tagged transgenic ribosomal subunit. key comparison IP fraction (immunoprecipitated, actively translated transcripts) INPUT fraction (total RNA — transcripts). Genes significantly enriched IP relative INPUT translated tagged cell type. PhosphoTRAP adds activity-marking phosphorylation step, recently activated neurons captured — combining cell-type specificity behavioural state information single experiment.

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("laurenoconnelllab/pTRAPPING")

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Throughout README simulate mouse TRAP-seq experiment studying pair-bonding preoptic area (POA) hypothalamus — brain region well-known role social behaviour. Mice divided two groups: nb – non-bonded control mice pb – pair-bonded mice (48 h co-housing partner) group 3 animals (biological replicates). animal contributes one IP one INPUT library, giving 12 samples total. column-naming convention <treatment><replicate><fraction> (e.g. nb1INPUT, pb2IP) lets ptrap_de() parse sample metadata automatically — sample_df needed. first column holds gene identifiers; remaining 12 columns raw counts. Snap25 (neuronal) already shows high IP counts groups, Oxtr (pair-bonding marker) shows balanced IP/INPUT — enriched pb.
code: library(pTRAPPING) library(ggplot2)  set.seed(42)  n_genes  <- 2000 gene_ids <- paste0("Gene", seq_len(n_genes))  # ── Replace early positions with real mouse gene names ────────────────────── # Neuronal markers — expected to be enriched in IP in BOTH groups neuronal   <- c("Snap25", "Rbfox3", "Camk2a", "Vglut1", "Syn1",                 "Map2",   "Nrgn",   "Bdnf",   "Pvalb",  "Sst") # Pair-bonding markers — enriched in IP only in pb pb_markers <- c("Oxtr", "Avpr1a", "Fos", "Arc", "Egr1") # Glial markers — NOT enriched in neuronal IP (control transcripts) glial      <- c("Gfap", "Aldh1l1", "S100b", "Cx3cr1", "Tmem119")  gene_ids[seq_along(neuronal)]                                       <- neuronal gene_ids[length(neuronal) + seq_along(pb_markers)]                  <- pb_markers gene_ids[length(neuronal) + length(pb_markers) + seq_along(glial)]  <- glial  # ── True IP / INPUT enrichment ratios ─────────────────────────────────────── base_enrich        <- rep(1.0, n_genes) base_enrich[1:10]  <- 8   # neuronal markers — constitutively enriched in both groups base_enrich[11:15] <- 1   # pair-bonding markers — baseline (no enrichment in nb)  pb_extra           <- rep(1.0, n_genes) pb_extra[11:15]    <- 8   # pair-bonding markers strongly enriched only in pb  # ── Helper: simulate one IP/INPUT pair for all genes ──────────────────────── sim_pair <- function(enrich, mu = 200, size = 10, sd_noise = 0.15) {   input <- rnbinom(n_genes, mu = mu, size = size)   ip    <- pmax(round(input * enrich * exp(rnorm(n_genes, 0, sd_noise))), 0L)   list(input = input, ip = ip) }  # ── Build counts data frame with auto-parseable column names ──────────────── cols <- lapply(1:3, function(i) {   nb <- sim_pair(base_enrich)   pb <- sim_pair(base_enrich * pb_extra)   setNames(     data.frame(nb$input, nb$ip, pb$input, pb$ip),     c(paste0("nb", i, "INPUT"), paste0("nb", i, "IP"),       paste0("pb", i, "INPUT"), paste0("pb", i, "IP"))   ) })  counts_mat <- data.frame(Gene = gene_ids, do.call(cbind, cols))  # Preview — neuronal marker, pair-bonding marker, glial marker counts_mat[c(1, 11, 16), 1:7] #>      Gene nb1INPUT nb1IP pb1INPUT pb1IP nb2INPUT nb2IP #> 1  Snap25      274  2231      150   910      229  1467 #> 11   Oxtr      217   260       83   630      290   333 #> 16   Gfap      151   117      143   123      222   307

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: sample_df omitted, ptrap_de() reads treatment, replicate number, fraction directly column names. Providing treatment_name tells function group analyse: neuronal markers (Snap25, Camk2a, etc.) enriched IP groups. pair-bonding markers (Oxtr, Avpr1a, Fos, Arc, Egr1) appear pb result — exactly expected activity-dependent transcripts pair-bonded neurons.
code: res_nb <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "LRT"        # edgeR likelihood ratio test ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # Top enriched genes in nb head(res_nb[res_nb$diffexpressed != "NO",             c("Gene", "logFC", "LR", "PValue", "FDR", "diffexpressed")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   Gene   logFC    LR    PValue       FDR diffexpressed #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 Sst     2.95  695. 3.27e-153 6.54e-150 UP            #> 2 Syn1    3.00  693. 1.10e-152 1.10e-149 UP            #> 3 Snap25  2.90  656. 1.40e-144 9.31e-142 UP            #> 4 Vglut1  3.01  631. 3.12e-139 1.56e-136 UP            #> 5 Bdnf    2.92  624. 1.13e-137 4.50e-135 UP            #> 6 Rbfox3  2.79  616. 5.50e-136 1.83e-133 UP res_pb <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "LRT" ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # Top enriched genes in pb head(res_pb[res_pb$diffexpressed != "NO",             c("Gene", "logFC", "LR", "PValue", "FDR", "diffexpressed")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   Gene  logFC    LR    PValue       FDR diffexpressed #>   <chr> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 Egr1   3.23  629. 6.88e-139 1.38e-135 UP            #> 2 Map2   3.12  600. 1.63e-132 1.63e-129 UP            #> 3 Arc    3.13  591. 1.60e-130 1.07e-127 UP            #> 4 Syn1   2.98  550. 1.36e-121 6.78e-119 UP            #> 5 Sst    2.94  543. 4.18e-120 1.67e-117 UP            #> 6 Nrgn   2.99  542. 5.61e-120 1.87e-117 UP

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Note "paired.ttest": GLM-based methods ("LRT" / "QLF") estimate gene-wise dispersion data work best ≥ 4 replicates. n = 3, dispersion estimates unreliable. paired t-test avoids using within-gene variance across pairs, following Tan et al. (2016).
code: # Quasi-likelihood F-test — more conservative than LRT res_qlf <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "QLF" )  # Paired t-test — recommended for real PhosphoTRAP data with n = 3 animals # Follows the approach in Tan et al. 2016 (Cell 167, 47-59) res_pt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest" )

what: Long-format paired data with return_long = TRUE
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: return_long = TRUE ("paired.ttest") returns per-gene, per-animal paired table used internally — useful plotting individual data points performing additional quality checks:
code: res_long <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest",   return_long    = TRUE ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # DE results tibble head(res_long$results[, c("Gene", "logFC", "t_statistic", "PValue", "FDR")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   Gene      logFC t_statistic   PValue   FDR #>   <chr>     <dbl>       <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Gene1833  0.144        65   0.000237 0.473 #> 2 Pvalb     2.91         39.4 0.000645 0.645 #> 3 Gene1001 -0.197       -27.6 0.00131  0.772 #> 4 Gene68    0.134        24.5 0.00166  0.772 #> 5 Gene248   0.221        19.7 0.00257  0.772 #> 6 Gene1529  0.211        19.0 0.00275  0.772  # Per-gene, per-animal paired counts table (showing Snap25) res_long$long_data[res_long$long_data$Gene == "Snap25", ] #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   Gene   block ip_count input_count #>   <chr>  <chr>    <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 Snap25 1         2231         274 #> 2 Snap25 2         1467         229 #> 3 Snap25 3         1398         177

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Adjust lfc_threshold fdr_threshold control strict DE classification :
code: # Relaxed: any enrichment above 0.5 log2FC at FDR < 0.10 res_relaxed <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "LRT",   lfc_threshold  = 0.5,   fdr_threshold  = 0.10 ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  table(res_relaxed$diffexpressed) #>  #>   NO   UP  #> 1984   16

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Set kable.= TRUE get formatted HTML table top genes — ideal Quarto / R Markdown reports:
code: ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "LRT",   kable.out      = TRUE,   ngenes.out     = 10 )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: classic volcano plot one treatment condition, significant genes colour-coded labelled:  Customise colours thresholds:  Oxtr, Avpr1a, Fos, Arc, Egr1 appear pb plot — consistent known roles pair-bond formation neuronal immediate-early gene expression.
code: ptrap_volcano(   res_nb,   title = "Non-bonded (nb) — POA" ) ptrap_volcano(   res_pb,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   colors        = c("UP" = "#E69F00", "DOWN" = "#56B4E9"),   title         = "Pair-bonded (pb) — POA" )

what: ptrap_volcano2() — dual scatter comparing two treatments
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: ptrap_volcano2() overlays treatment conditions single scatter plot. axis shows log₂ fold-change (IP / INPUT) one group, genes colour-coded DE status across conditions:  read plot * Top-right quadrant: enriched IP groups → constitutive neuronal translation (e.g. Snap25, Camk2a, Rbfox3). * Bottom-right: enriched pb → activity-dependent translation pair-bonded animals (Oxtr, Fos, Arc, Egr1, Avpr1a). * Top-left: enriched nb → non-bonded-specific. * dotted diagonal identity line — equal enrichment groups. Customise colours thresholds:
code: ptrap_volcano2(   res_nb, res_pb,   treatment_col = "Treatment",   title         = "POA — nb vs pb" ) ptrap_volcano2(   res_nb, res_pb,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   treatment_col = "Treatment",   title         = "POA — nb vs pb",   colors        = c(     "DE in both"   = "#D55E00",     "DE only nb"   = "#0072B2",     "DE only pb"   = "#009E73"   ) )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: limma_de() wraps limma linear modelling framework continuous log-scale data (label-free proteomics, metabolomics, RNA-seq voom). simulate three-group proteomics experiment — control (ctrl), treatment (trtA), treatment B (trtB), n = 6 replicates . Tip — contrast string naming convention: Inside limma_de(), design matrix built model.matrix(~ 0 + group) (pairwise) model.matrix(~ group) (reference), variable always called group. Coefficient names therefore follow pattern group<level> (e.g. groupctrl, grouptrtA). Pass contrast strings using names.
code: set.seed(123)  n_prot <- 1000 n_rep  <- 6 groups <- factor(rep(c("ctrl", "trtA", "trtB"), each = n_rep))  # log2 protein abundances drawn from a normal background prot_mat <- matrix(   rnorm(n_prot * length(groups), mean = 10, sd = 0.4),   nrow     = n_prot,   dimnames = list(     paste0("Prot", seq_len(n_prot)),     paste0(rep(c("ctrl", "trtA", "trtB"), each = n_rep),            "_", seq_len(length(groups)))   ) )  # Inject true biological differences prot_mat[1:60,    groups == "trtA"]               <- prot_mat[1:60,    groups == "trtA"]               + 2 prot_mat[61:120,  groups == "trtB"]               <- prot_mat[61:120,  groups == "trtB"]               - 2 prot_mat[121:150, groups %in% c("trtA", "trtB")]  <- prot_mat[121:150, groups %in% c("trtA", "trtB")]  + 2

what: Pairwise design with eBayes() (standard empirical Bayes)
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: element $de_list tibble one contrast:
code: library(limma)  res_prot <- limma_de(   expr_mat      = prot_mat,   group         = groups,   contrast_mat  = c(trtA_vs_ctrl = "grouptrtA - groupctrl",                     trtB_vs_ctrl = "grouptrtB - groupctrl"),   test_method   = "eBayes",   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05 )  # Number of DE proteins per contrast sapply(res_prot$de_list, nrow) #> trtA_vs_ctrl trtB_vs_ctrl  #>           90           90 # Proteins DE in opposite directions across contrasts (worth investigating!) res_prot$shared #> # A tibble: 0 × 4 #> # ℹ 4 variables: feature <chr>, min_fc <dbl>, max_fc <dbl>, n_contrasts <int>  # Consistently DE proteins, counted by direction res_prot$unique #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   direction n_features #>   <chr>          <int> #> 1 DOWN              60 #> 2 UP                90 head(res_prot$de_list[["trtA_vs_ctrl"]]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 9 #>   feature logFC AveExpr     t  P.Value adj.P.Val     B contrast    diffexpressed #>   <chr>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> <chr>       <chr>         #> 1 Prot25   2.57    10.6  11.2 3.37e-25  3.37e-22  46.5 trtA_vs_ct… UP            #> 2 Prot48   2.51    10.6  11.0 1.22e-24  6.11e-22  45.2 trtA_vs_ct… UP            #> 3 Prot22   2.47    10.6  10.8 9.22e-24  3.07e-21  43.2 trtA_vs_ct… UP            #> 4 Prot38   2.45    10.7  10.6 3.23e-23  8.07e-21  42.0 trtA_vs_ct… UP            #> 5 Prot46   2.39    10.6  10.5 6.09e-23  1.03e-20  41.4 trtA_vs_ct… UP            #> 6 Prot13   2.42    10.7  10.5 6.19e-23  1.03e-20  41.3 trtA_vs_ct… UP

what: Pairwise design with treat() (minimum fold-change test)
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: treat() tests whether |logFC| exceeds lfc_threshold, providing stronger protection proteins statistical biological significance. Recommended biological effect sizes well-defined (e.g. proteomics):
code: res_prot_tr <- limma_de(   expr_mat      = prot_mat,   group         = groups,   contrast_mat  = c(trtA_vs_ctrl = "grouptrtA - groupctrl",                     trtB_vs_ctrl = "grouptrtB - groupctrl"),   test_method   = "treat",   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05 )  sapply(res_prot_tr$de_list, nrow) #> trtA_vs_ctrl trtB_vs_ctrl  #>           86           87

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: comparisons single baseline, model_type = "reference" simpler alternative — first factor level becomes reference coefficients differences :
code: res_prot_ref <- limma_de(   expr_mat      = prot_mat,   group         = as.character(groups),   contrast_mat  = c("grouptrtA", "grouptrtB"),   # coefficient names in ~ group   model_type    = "reference",   test_method   = "eBayes",   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05 ) #> Renaming (Intercept) to Intercept  sapply(res_prot_ref$de_list, nrow) #> grouptrtA grouptrtB  #>        90        90

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
code: limma_de(   expr_mat      = prot_mat,   group         = groups,   contrast_mat  = c(trtA_vs_ctrl = "grouptrtA - groupctrl",                     trtB_vs_ctrl = "grouptrtB - groupctrl"),   test_method   = "eBayes",   lfc_threshold = 1,   kable_out     = TRUE,   ngenes_out    = 10,   kable_coef    = 1      # display: trtA vs ctrl )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: complex designs — multiple brain regions, custom blocking variables, non-standard column naming — supply sample_df directly:
code: # Example: two brain regions, two treatments sample_df <- data.frame(   sample    = c("POA_nb1_IP", "POA_nb1_INPUT", "POA_pb1_IP", "POA_pb1_INPUT",                 "AMY_nb1_IP", "AMY_nb1_INPUT", "AMY_pb1_IP", "AMY_pb1_INPUT"),   region    = rep(c("POA", "AMY"), each = 4),   treatment = rep(c("nb", "nb", "pb", "pb"), 2),   block     = rep("1", 8),   fraction  = rep(c("IP", "INPUT", "IP", "INPUT"), 2) )  res_poa_pb <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = my_counts,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = my_gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   region_col     = "region",   treatment_col  = "treatment",   block_col      = "block",   fraction_col   = "fraction",   test_method    = "paired.ttest" )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: use pTRAPPING research, please cite: Rodríguez, C. (2024). pTRAPPING: Suite Functions Analyses PhosphoTRAP Data R. https://github.com/laurenoconnelllab/pTRAPPING paired t-test method (test_method = "paired.ttest"): Tan, C.L., Cooke, E.K., Leib, D.E., Lin, Y.C., Daly, G.E., Zimmerman, C.., Knight, Z.. (2016). Warm-Sensitive Neurons Control Body Temperature. Cell 167, 47–59. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028

what: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
text: Runs complete limma workflow feature--sample expression abundance matrix: builds design matrix group vector, fits linear model, applies contrasts, tests differential expression using either limma::treat() limma::eBayes(). Returns list containing per-contrast DE tables, combined table significant features, summary features inconsistent directions across contrasts, direction counts.

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
code: limma_de(   expr_mat,   group,   contrast_mat,   coefs = NULL,   model_type = c("pairwise", "reference"),   test_method = c("treat", "eBayes"),   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   feature_col = "feature",   ngenes_out = 20,   kable_out = FALSE,   kable_coef = 1 )

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
text: expr_mat numeric matrix features (genes / proteins) rows samples columns. Row names must feature identifiers column names must sample IDs. group character factor vector group labels, one element per column expr_mat. order must match columns expr_mat. contrast_mat contrast matrix produced limma::makeContrasts(), character vector contrast strings (e.g. c("groupA-groupB", "groupB-groupC")) passed limma::makeContrasts() internally. Contrast names must match column names design matrix (e.g. "groupA", "groupB", ...). coefs integer vector specifying columns contrast_mat extract results . NULL (default), contrasts used. model_type One "pairwise" (intercept, ~ 0 + group, recommended multi-group designs explicit contrasts) "reference" (first level reference, ~ group). See Choosing model_type section. Default "pairwise". test_method One "treat" "eBayes". See Choosing test_method section guidance. Default "treat". lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change. Passed treat(lfc = lfc_threshold) test_method = "treat", used filter threshold methods. Default 1. fdr_threshold Maximum adjusted p-value feature classified differentially expressed. Default 0.05. feature_col Name column hold feature identifiers (.e. row names expr_mat) output tables. Default "feature". ngenes_out Number top features display kable_out = TRUE. Default 20. kable_out Logical. TRUE, returns HTML kableExtra table single contrast (indexed kable_coef) instead full list. Default FALSE. kable_coef Integer giving position within coefs contrast display kable_out = TRUE. Default 1 (first selected contrast).

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
text: kable_out = FALSE (default), named list : de_list named list tibbles, one per selected contrast, containing DE features (filtered lfc_threshold fdr_threshold). tibble includes feature_col, logFC, adj.P.Val, contrast, diffexpressed ("" ""). all_de per-contrast DE tibbles de_list combined one tibble dplyr::bind_rows(). feature appears per contrast significant. shared Tibble features DE opposite directions across different contrasts (min logFC < 0 max logFC > 0). Useful flagging features whose direction change consistent. unique Tibble counting consistently DE features direction ("" "") — features absent shared. fit fitted MArrayLM object returned treat() eBayes(), downstream use (e.g. calling topTreat() topTable() additional contrast columns). kable_out = TRUE, HTML kableExtra table top ngenes_out features selected contrast.

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
text: "pairwise" Fits model.matrix(~ 0 + group). intercept; every group gets coefficient. Use want explicit pairwise contrasts (e.g. groupA - groupB). Requires contrast matrix. "reference" Fits model.matrix(~ group). first factor level intercept / reference group; coefficients represent differences . Simpler one natural reference condition comparisons.

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
text: "treat" Calls limma::treat(), tests minimum fold-change threshold (lfc_threshold) rather zero. Recommended proteomics metabolomics data want ensure biological well statistical significance. Results extracted limma::topTreat(). "eBayes" Calls limma::eBayes(), standard empirical Bayes moderation. Tests null logFC = 0. Appropriate RNA-seq (voom) analysis minimum fold-change required. Results extracted limma::topTable().

title: Differential expression analysis using limma — limma_de
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # --- pairwise design with treat() (proteomics / metabolomics) --- group_vec <- rep(c("groupA", "groupB", "groupC"), each = 3)  # build the contrast matrix for selected pairwise comparisons df_design <- data.frame(group = factor(group_vec)) design_mat <- model.matrix(~ 0 + group, data = df_design) cont_mat <- limma::makeContrasts(   groupA - groupB,   groupA - groupC,   levels = design_mat )  res <- limma_de(   expr_mat      = my_matrix,   group         = group_vec,   contrast_mat  = cont_mat,   coefs         = 1:2,   test_method   = "treat",   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05 )  res$de_list    # named list, one tibble per contrast res$all_de     # all contrasts combined res$shared  # features with inconsistent direction res$unique  # count of consistently UP / DOWN features  # --- single comparison, reference group design with eBayes() --- res2 <- limma_de(   expr_mat     = my_matrix,   group        = group_vec,   contrast_mat = "groupgroupB",   # coefficient name in ~ group design   model_type   = "reference",   test_method  = "eBayes" ) } # }

what: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR or a paired t-test — ptrap_de
title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR or a paired t-test — ptrap_de
text: Compares IP vs INPUT fractions specified treatment condition using one three statistical approaches (see test_method). sample_df gene_ids optional: function can derive sample metadata automatically column names counts_mat, gene identifiers first column.

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR or a paired t-test — ptrap_de
code: ptrap_de(   counts_mat,   sample_df = NULL,   gene_ids = NULL,   region_name = NULL,   treatment_name = NULL,   sample_col = "sample",   fraction_col = "fraction",   block_col = "tube",   region_col = NULL,   treatment_col = "Treatment",   ip_level = "IP",   input_level = "INPUT",   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   test_method = c("LRT", "QLF", "paired.ttest"),   pseudocount = 1,   return_long = FALSE,   ngenes.out = 20,   kable.out = FALSE )

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR or a paired t-test — ptrap_de
text: counts_mat counts matrix one two formats: Matrix — numeric, genes × samples; column names sample IDs; gene IDs rownames supplied via gene_ids. Data frame / tibble — first column character vector gene IDs; remaining columns numeric counts sample names column names. sample_df = NULL, column names must follow naming convention described Automatic column-name parsing section. sample_df Optional data frame sample metadata. NULL (default), metadata parsed automatically column names counts_mat. provided, arguments sample_col, fraction_col, block_col, region_col, treatment_col specify columns use (falling back defaults names match). gene_ids Optional character vector gene identifiers corresponding rows counts_mat. NULL (default), gene IDs extracted first column counts_mat (data frame) rownames(counts_mat) (matrix). region_name brain region subset analyze (e.g., "POA"). used region_col NULL. Can NULL region_col = NULL (default) data contain single region. treatment_name treatment condition whose samples subsetted IP vs INPUT comparison (e.g., "pb" analyse pair-bonded samples). Note treatment_name DE contrast variable — contrast always IP vs INPUT. compare two treatments call ptrap_de() per treatment pass results ptrap_volcano2(). NULL (default), value derived automatically sample_df (parsed column names sample_df = NULL): single treatment found function proceeds silently; multiple treatments found error asks user specify one analyse. sample_col Name column sample_df whose values match column names counts_mat. Ignored sample_df = NULL (auto-set internally). Default "sample". fraction_col Name column sample_df distinguishes IP INPUT fractions. Default "fraction". block_col Name column sample_df used blocking / pairing variable (e.g., individual animal tube). "LRT" / "QLF" enters design matrix; "paired.ttest" aligns animal's IP INPUT. Default "tube". region_col Name column sample_df containing brain region labels. Set NULL (default) skip region filtering — recommended data already contain one brain region. set counts_mat contains multiple regions want analyze one time. Default NULL. treatment_col Name column sample_df containing treatment labels. Default "Treatment". ip_level value fraction_col identifies IP fraction. Default "IP". input_level value fraction_col identifies INPUT fraction (reference level). Default "INPUT". lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change required classify gene differentially expressed. Default 1. fdr_threshold Maximum FDR (adjusted p-value "paired.ttest") allowed classify gene differentially expressed. Default 0.05. test_method Statistical method use. One : "LRT" — likelihood ratio test via glmFit + glmLRT (default). Suitable ≥ 4 replicates raw count data. "QLF" — quasi-likelihood F-test via glmQLFit + glmQLFTest. conservative LRT; recommended small sample sizes raw count data. "paired.ttest" — per-gene paired t-test IP INPUT across replicates, following Tan et al. (2016) doi:10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028 . Best suited n = 3 replicates typical PhosphoTRAP experiments. P-values adjusted Benjamini-Hochberg. enrichment ratio per paired sample (IP/INPUT) expressed log2 scale. pseudocount small value added counts computing log2(IP/INPUT) "paired.ttest", avoid log(0). Default 1. Set 0 counts_mat already contains normalised values (e.g., RPKM/TPM) guaranteed > 0. return_long Logical. used test_method = "paired.ttest". TRUE, returns named list $results (DE tibble) $long_data (per-gene, per-animal paired table used test). Default FALSE. ngenes.Number top genes (sorted p-value) include output kable.= TRUE. Default 20. kable.Logical. TRUE, returns kableExtra HTML table top ngenes.genes instead full tibble. Default FALSE.

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR or a paired t-test — ptrap_de
text: kable.= FALSE (default), tibble one row per gene sorted p-value, containing Gene, logFC, test-statistic column(s), PValue, FDR, treatment label, optionally region label (region_col set), diffexpressed ("", "", ""). kable.= TRUE, HTML kableExtra table top ngenes.genes. "paired.ttest" return_long = TRUE, named list $results $long_data.

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR or a paired t-test — ptrap_de
text: "LRT" "QLF" use edgeR's GLM framework, account paired animal structure via block variable, recommended 4 replicates raw count data. "paired.ttest" runs per-gene paired t-test IP INPUT counts across experimental repeats, following method described Tan et al. (2016) Cell 167, 47–59 doi:10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028 . approach recommended 3 replicates (typical minimum PhosphoTRAP experiments), GLM-based dispersion estimates unreliable. enrichment ratio (IP / INPUT) computed per paired sample expressed log2 scale, described paper. Note: pass normalised values (e.g., RPKM/TPM) instead raw counts, set pseudocount = 0.

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR or a paired t-test — ptrap_de
text: sample_df = NULL, column names counts_mat (excluding first, gene-ID column) parsed build sample metadata automatically. column name must encode three pieces information, order combination separators (_, -, ., space) separator : Treatment One letters identifying experimental group. Replicate number digit identifying biological replicate. Fraction ip_level input_level (case-insensitive); "" also accepted short alias INPUT fraction. Valid column name examples (default ip_level = "IP", input_level = "INPUT"):

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR or a paired t-test — ptrap_de
text: Tan, C.L., Cooke, E.K., Leib, D.E., Lin, Y.C., Daly, G.E., Zimmerman, C.., Knight, Z.. (2016). Warm-Sensitive Neurons Control Body Temperature. Cell 167, 47–59. doi:10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028

title: Perform differential expression analysis for TRAP-seq data using edgeR or a paired t-test — ptrap_de
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## ---- Option A: simplest call — auto-parse from column names --------------- # counts_mat is a data frame where: #   col 1      = gene IDs #   col 2+     = samples named like "b1input", "b1ip", "nb2input", etc. counts <- read.table("counts.txt", header = TRUE)  # single treatment in the matrix — treatment_name auto-detected res <- ptrap_de(counts_mat = counts, test_method = "paired.ttest")  # multiple treatments — specify which one to analyze res_b <- ptrap_de(counts_mat = counts, treatment_name = "b")  ## ---- Option B: provide sample_df explicitly (original workflow) ----------- res_lrt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb" )  # quasi-likelihood F-test res_qlf <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "QLF" )  # paired t-test — also return long-format paired table res_pt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest",   return_long    = TRUE ) res_pt$results    # DE tibble res_pt$long_data  # per-gene, per-animal pairs } # }

what: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: Takes single tibble produced ptrap_de(), limma_de() similar returns classic volcano plot (logFC x-axis, -log10(FDR) y-axis). Non-significant genes shown grey; genes classified "" "" diffexpressed column highlighted distinct fill colours labelled gene identifier. Dashed threshold lines drawn ±lfc_threshold (vertical) -log10(fdr_threshold) (horizontal).

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
code: ptrap_volcano(   de_result,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   gene_col = "Gene",   treatment_col = "Treatment",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3.5,   point_alpha = 0.7,   max_overlaps = 20,   title = NULL )

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: de_result tibble returned ptrap_de(), containing minimum columns Gene (gene_col), logFC, FDR, diffexpressed. lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change used draw vertical threshold lines. match value used ptrap_de(). Default 1. fdr_threshold FDR cutoff used draw horizontal threshold line (plotted -log10(fdr_threshold)). match value used ptrap_de(). Default 0.05. gene_col Name column containing gene identifiers. Default "Gene". treatment_col Name column containing treatment label. Used build default plot title. Default "Treatment". region_col Name column containing brain region label. Used build default plot title. Default "BrainRegion". colors named character vector mapping "" "" colours. NULL (default), colourblind-friendly palette used ("#D55E00" "", "#0072B2" ""). point_size Size points. Default 3.5. point_alpha Opacity highlighted (significant) points. Default 0.7. max_overlaps Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Controls many overlapping labels suppressed. Default 20. title Plot title. NULL (default), title auto-generated brain region treatment labels found de_result.

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb" )  # Default plot ptrap_volcano(res)  # Custom thresholds and colours ptrap_volcano(res,               lfc_threshold = 0.5,               fdr_threshold = 0.1,               colors        = c("UP" = "#E69F00", "DOWN" = "#56B4E9")) } # }

what: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: Takes two tibbles produced ptrap_de() similar — one per treatment condition, brain region — joins gene, classifies gene according differential expression status condition, returns scatter plot logFC\(_{\text{treatment 1}}\) vs logFC\(_{\text{treatment 2}}\). Significant genes highlighted labelled; threshold lines drawn ±lfc_threshold axes.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
code: ptrap_volcano2(   de_result_1,   de_result_2,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   gene_col = "Gene",   treatment_col = "Treatment",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3.5,   point_alpha = 0.7,   max_overlaps = 20,   title = NULL )

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: de_result_1 tibble returned ptrap_de() first treatment condition (plotted y-axis). de_result_2 tibble returned ptrap_de() second treatment condition (plotted x-axis). lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change used define significance. Must match (stricter ) threshold used ptrap_de(). Default 1. fdr_threshold Maximum FDR used define significance. Default 0.05. gene_col Name column containing gene identifiers result tibbles. Default "Gene". treatment_col Name column containing treatment label result tibbles. Used auto-generate axis labels DE class names. Default "Treatment". region_col Name column containing brain region label. Used set default plot title. Default "BrainRegion". colors named character vector mapping DE class colour. Names must "DE ", "DE <t1>", "DE <t2>", <t1> / <t2> treatment names found data. NULL (default), colourblind-friendly palette used. point_size Size points. Default 3.5. point_alpha Opacity coloured (significant) points. Default 0.7. max_overlaps Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Controls many overlapping labels allowed hidden. Default 20. title Plot title. NULL (default), brain region name extracted de_result_1 used.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res_pb  <- ptrap_de(counts_mat, sample_df, gene_ids,                     region_name = "POA", treatment_name = "pb") res_sol <- ptrap_de(counts_mat, sample_df, gene_ids,                     region_name = "POA", treatment_name = "sol")  # Default plot — treatment 1 on y-axis, treatment 2 on x-axis ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol)  # Custom thresholds, title and colours ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol,                lfc_threshold = 0.5,                fdr_threshold = 0.1,                title         = "POA: pb vs sol",                colors        = c("DE in both"  = "#D55E00",                                  "DE only pb"  = "#0072B2",                                  "DE only sol" = "#49a15f")) } # }
